key_insight: Unsupervised style-transfer domain adaptation can improve calcification classification generalization across mammography sites and vendors.
clinical_relevance: This approach could help CAD systems stay more reliable when deployed on images from hospitals and scanner types not seen during training.
method: Used AdaIN and CycleGAN to create vendor- and technique-specific training samples without extra labels.
Trained a supervised classifier with Swin Transformer V2 as the backbone.
Compared performance across multi-site mammography datasets with external validation.
evidence_signal: The model was cross-validated on OPTIMAM (n=2994) and externally validated on EMBED (n=125) and Duke Calcification Dataset v1 (n=788), improving AUC from 0.68 to 0.72 on EMBED and from 0.68 to 0.73 on Duke.
watch_item: Improvements were modest, so real-world deployment still needs broader prospective validation across additional sites, vendors, and imaging protocols.
paper_title: Unsupervised Domain Adaptation for Calcification Classification in Mammography Across Multi-Site Datasets
